questionHtml: The work done during the isothermal expansion A → B is 540 J. Calculate the thermal energy that leaves the gas during one cycle.
markschemeHtml:
heat into gas «is along AB» and equals

Qin «= ΔU + W = 0 + 540» = 540 «J» ✔

heat out is (1−e) Qin = (1−0.45) × 540 = 297 «J» ≈ 3.0 × 102 «J» ✔

Award [2] for bald correct answer.

meta.title: The work done during the isothermal expansion A → B is 540 J. Calculate the thermal energy that leaves the gas during one cycle.
